publication/title: PhenoMATRIX™ for the screening of Group B Streptococcus (GBS) carriage in pregnant women: ready to get rid of the LIM broth?
publication/authors: Cherkaoui, A; Renzi, G; Schrenzel, J
publication/journal: European Journal of Clinical Microbiology & Infectious Diseases

dataset/provenance:
* **Data Source Name**:
  * Training Source: Culture images of 8,768 CA media used for direct plating and the subculture of the LIM broth.
  * Validation Source: 830 CA media, which corresponded to 437 non-duplicate vagino-rectal ESwab specimens sequentially referred to the laboratory plus 393 additional CA from the LIM broth subcultures.

* **Total Data Points**:
  * Training Data Points: N=8,768
  * Validation Data Points: N=830

* **Previous Use**: No. Not enough information is available.

dataset/redundancy:
* **Redundancy Methodology**: The study used sequential sampling to ensure non-redundancy between splits. The training phase utilized 8,768 culture images of CA media, while the validation phase used 830 CA media from non-duplicate vagino-rectal ESwab specimens and additional CA from LIM broth subcultures. This sequential approach aimed to prevent overlap between the training and validation datasets.

* **Independent Sets**: Yes. The study explicitly mentions that the validation phase was carried out on a separate set of 830 CA media, which were not part of the training dataset. This indicates that the training and test sets were strictly independent.

* **Independence Enforcement**: The independence was enforced by using sequential sampling, where the validation set consisted of CA media from non-duplicate vagino-rectal ESwab specimens and additional CA from LIM broth subcultures that were not included in the training set. This method ensured that there was no overlap between the training and validation datasets.

optimization/encoding: * **Data Encoding Method**: The raw data consisted of high-resolution digital images of chromogenic agar (CA) media taken at different incubation times (0h, 18h, and 24h). These images were assessed for the presence of pale pink to red round and pearly colonies indicative of Group B Streptococcus (GBS). The images were classified by manual work-up as either GBS-positive or GBS-negative, serving as the ground truth for training the convolutional neural networks (CNNs). This classification process can be considered a form of label encoding, where the images are tagged with binary labels (GBS-positive or GBS-negative). The images were then used to train the CNNs, which likely involved converting the pixel values of the images into a format suitable for neural network input, such as normalization or standardization of pixel intensities.

evaluation/method:
* **Evaluation Strategy**:
  * **Independent hold-out sets**: The validation phase was carried out on 830 CA media, which corresponded to 437 non-duplicate vagino-rectal ESwab specimens and 393 additional CA from the LIM broth subcultures. Digital images of the CA were prospectively analyzed by PhenoMATRIX™. The same CA images were read by the technologists blinded to the results returned by PhenoMATRIX™. All the discrepant results (n = 71) were confirmed by clinical microbiologists by reassessing the digital images.
evaluation/measure:
**Performance Metrics:**

* **Direct CA Plating vs. Manual Work-up:**
  * Sensitivity: 100% (95% CI, 95.9%—100%)
  * Specificity: 88.2% (95% CI, 84.3%–91.1%)
  * Positive Predictive Value: 55.8% (95% CI, 48.6%—62.7%)
  * Negative Predictive Value: 100% (95% CI, 98.8%—100%)

* **LIM-broth Enriched Plating on CA vs. Manual Work-up:**
  * Sensitivity: 100% (95% CI, 82.4%—100%)
  * Specificity: 92.0% (95% CI, 88.8%—94.3%)
  * Positive Predictive Value: 65.1% (95% CI, 57%—72.5%)
  * Negative Predictive Value: 100% (95% CI, 98.9%—100%)

* **Direct CA Plating and LIM-broth Enriched Plating on CA vs. Manual Work-up:**
  * Sensitivity: 100% (95% CI, 96.6% - 100%)
  * Specificity: 90.2% (95% CI, 87.8% - 92.1%)
  * Positive Predictive Value: 60.3% (95% CI, 54.9% - 65.4%)
  * Negative Predictive Value: 100% (95% CI, 99.4% - 100%)

* **Direct CA Plating vs. LIM Enrichment broth subcultured on CA:**
  * Sensitivity: 98.3% (95% CI, 97.3%—98.9%)
  * Specificity: 99.7% (95% CI, 99.5%—99.8%)
  * Positive Predictive Value: 97.9% (95% CI, 96.9%—98.6%)
  * Negative Predictive Value: 99.7% (95% CI, 99.6%—99.8%)
evaluation/comparison:
* **Comparison Performed**: Yes
  * **List of tools/baselines**:
    * **Manual Reading**: Manual work-up of chromID™ Strepto B agar (CA) plates.
    * **LIM Enrichment Broth**: LIM broth enrichment with subsequent subculture on CA.
    * **Direct CA Plating**: Direct plating on chromID™ Strepto B agar (CA).
    * **Trans-Vag Broth**: Todd-Hewitt broth with 8 µg/ml gentamicin and 15 µg/ml nalidixic acid (Trans-Vag broth) subcultured on 5% sheep blood agar (SBA).
    * **ChromID™ Strepto B Agar (CA)**: Direct plating on CA.
    * **Columbia Colistin-Nalidixic Agar (CNA)**: Direct plating on CNA.
    * **Molecular Assays**: LAMP, qPCR.
    * **Total Laboratory Automation (TLA)**: WASPLab™ (Copan Diagnostics) with PhenoMATRIX™ software.
    * **Matrix-Assisted Laser Desorption Ionization–Time of Flight Mass Spectrometry (MALDI-TOF/MS)**: For confirmation of S. agalactiae.
